extraction.one_line_summary: VINGEGAARD Jonas attacked with 3km remaining on final climb summit, soloed to victory while BJERG crashed helping him and BERNAL attacked pursuit unsuccessfully.
tactical_patterns: Soudal-QuickStep chased hard early to control break for ALAPHILIPPE sprint, then reversed tactics on final climb showing team flexibility.
VINGEGAARD attacked exactly at summit transition point with 3km to climb, exploiting moment when chasers hesitate before descent.
BJERG crashed while protecting VINGEGAARD from counter-moves, suggesting Jumbo willing to sacrifice second GC card for stage win.
BERNAL attacked descent alone refusing group cooperation, tactical error that allowed organized chase group to limit losses.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: attacked summit with authority while rivals marked each other, showing confidence to go long solo effort
rider_signals.BJERG Mikkel: negative: crashed while blocking for VINGEGAARD during gear malfunction incident, compromising his own GC position
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: finished second after chasing hard suggests climbing form returning beyond expected sprint-only role
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: strongest descender who attacked alone but lacked cooperation skills to make decisive gap stick
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: bridged to VINGEGAARD on climb showing resurgent form but lacked firepower to sustain counter
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: neutral: mechanical in final 3km saved by commissaire ruling, masked whether he had legs to follow
rider_signals.LATOUR Pierre: negative: losing massive time on descents while climbing normally suggests psychological or injury issue with technical sections
